---
title: "InferenceController"
---

## Overview

**Header:** `DeepLearning_Widget/UI/InferenceController.hpp`

`InferenceController` orchestrates deep learning inference runs (single frame,
batch, multi-interval batch, and recurrent sequence). It owns the background
worker thread lifecycle and delegates result merging to `ResultProcessor`.

## Batch Inference

### Single Range

`runBatch(start, end, batch_size)` launches a `BatchInferenceWorker` on a
background `QThread`. The worker calls `SlotAssembler::runBatchRangeOffline()`
for the given frame range and reports progress via `batchProgressChanged(current, total)`.

### Multi-Interval Batch

`runBatchIntervals(intervals, batch_size)` accepts a vector of `(start, end)`
frame ranges (e.g. from a `DigitalIntervalSeries`) and processes each interval
sequentially on a single background thread. Progress is reported against the
**total** frame count across all intervals, so the progress bar reflects the
cumulative work (e.g. two intervals of 1000 frames reports progress out of 2000).

The `_onRunBatch()` dialog in `DeepLearningPropertiesWidget` offers two modes:

- **Manual range**: User specifies start/end frames directly (original behavior).
- **From interval series**: User selects a `DigitalIntervalSeries` key from
  DataManager. The combo box auto-populates and refreshes via a DataManager
  observer callback. The callback is cleaned up when the dialog is destroyed.

## Cancellation

All worker types share a `std::shared_ptr<std::atomic<bool>>` cancel flag owned
by the `InferenceController::Impl`. Calling `cancel()` sets this flag, which is
polled by `SlotAssembler::runBatchRangeOffline()` between batches. The same flag
is set in the destructor to ensure clean shutdown.

## Signal Chain

```
Worker::progressChanged(current, total)
    → InferenceController::batchProgressChanged(current, total)
        → DeepLearningPropertiesWidget::batchProgressChanged(current, total)
            → DeepLearningViewWidget::updateRecurrentProgress(current, total)
```
